specification.topic-statement: The tool data JSON builder constructs the structured JSON string stored in the data_json column of tool call and tool result database records.

scope.in-scope: Construction of tool call data records including thinking and redacted-thinking fields; construction of tool result data records including success derivation from the tool output string.
scope.boundary: The caller is responsible for supplying the produced JSON string to the database layer. How and when the record is persisted is out of scope.

The tool call and tool result inputs to the builder — identifiers, names, arguments, output strings, and thinking content — are produced by the tool execution pipeline. Their origin and meaning are out of scope.

field.name: tool_name
field.note: name of the invoked tool; always present

field.name: tool_args
field.note: tool argument JSON as a string value (not a nested object); always present

field.name: thinking
field.note: optional; present only when thinking text is non-null; contains 'text' (string, always present when object is included) and optionally 'signature' (string, present only when thinking signature is non-null)

field.name: redacted_thinking
field.note: optional; present only when redacted thinking data is non-null; contains 'data' (string)

field.name: tool_call_id
field.note: tool call identifier this result answers

field.name: output
field.note: the raw tool output string, stored verbatim

field.name: success
field.note: derived by parsing the output string as JSON and extracting the 'tool_success' boolean field; defaults to false if the output is not valid JSON, if parsing fails, or if the field is absent

behavior.tool-call-data-construction:
A JSON object is built from the provided tool call record. The three required fields — tool call identifier, tool name, and tool arguments — are always present as string values.
When thinking text is provided (non-null), a nested "thinking" object is appended to the root object. This nested object always contains the "text" field. If a thinking signature is also provided (non-null), a "signature" field is added to the nested object. If the signature is null, the "signature" field is omitted entirely.
When redacted thinking data is provided (non-null), a separate nested "redacted_thinking" object is appended to the root object, containing a single "data" field with the redacted content.
Neither "thinking" nor "redacted_thinking" appears in the output object when their respective inputs are null. Both may be present simultaneously, one, or neither, depending on what is supplied.
The result is serialized to a compact JSON string, copied into a talloc-allocated buffer owned by the provided talloc context, and returned. Any failure to allocate memory causes an immediate panic.
behavior.tool-result-data-construction:
A JSON object is built from the provided tool result inputs. The tool call identifier, tool name, and raw output string are always written as string fields.
The "success" boolean is derived by parsing the raw output string as JSON and looking up a top-level "tool_success" boolean field. If parsing fails (the string is not valid JSON), if the parsed document has no "tool_success" field, or if any other error occurs during extraction, the success value defaults to false. The success value is appended to the object after the output field.
The result is serialized, copied into a talloc-allocated buffer, and returned. Any allocation failure causes an immediate panic.
The caller does not supply a success flag. Success is always inferred by re-parsing the tool output. A tool output that is not valid JSON always produces success = false, regardless of the execution outcome.
